source: AGENTS.md section 9 (distributed test constitution), TECHNICAL_STANDARDS.md section 10.2
shard_method_note: No historical per-app duration data exists yet. Split is deterministic by test-collection count (discovery only, zero tests executed, zero DB writes) via django.test.utils.get_runner(settings)().build_suite([label]).countTestCases() per app. Refine to historical-duration once a full-suite run publishes durations, per AGENTS.md section 9 rule 'Split by historical duration when timing data exists.'
test_collection_method: Django DiscoverRunner.build_suite() called per app label, summed; cross-checked against a single build_suite([]) full-repo discovery call, both returned 1429. No test executed, no database created or written to for this count.

machine_map_resolution.resolved_by: owner, direct instruction, 2026-07-20; re-mapped 2026-07-23 after roster change
machine_map_resolution.reassignment_2026_07_23: Bolt and Ember are on weekly limits. Owner re-mapped the pool: Cursor now sits on Bolt's former machine (DESKTOP-ODD5QN8, the primary 8-core) — Bolt will relocate to a different computer when he returns from limit. GreenBear keeps the secondary 6-core. The linode_1_core is the production Linode itself (80.85.84.156) — it may still take a shard, run by either active agent over SSH, but kept the LIGHTEST/proportionally-smallest share because it is slower than the workstations AND serves live production during the run. Even wall-clock split is the goal. Constitution is now v1.3.0.

assignment.linode_1_core: Cursor or GreenBear (shared production Linode; lightest apps; test DB is separate, cache is locmem under IS_TESTING, so no file-cache risk)

machine_map_resolution.note: Role assignment is by owner decision, not re-derived from the live nproc evidence gathered earlier in this session. That evidence (GreenBear's own machine reported 8 logical cores via nproc, not 6; Bolt never reported a core count; Bolt and Ember reported the same hostname DESKTOP-ODD5QN8) is preserved below for the record, not overwritten -- the owner's assignment is authoritative regardless of the underlying hardware headroom.

primary_8_core.machine_id: DESKTOP-ODD5QN8 (per Bolt's bootstrap; logical core count not independently confirmed by Bolt this session)

secondary_6_core.machine_id_note: Confirmed via direct nproc on this machine: 8 logical cores, not 6. Recorded as evidence, not acted on -- shard content is sized to the 6/15 target share per the owner's role assignment above, independent of this machine's actual idle CPU headroom.

linode_1_core.machine_id_note: Confirmed via direct SSH: nproc=1, /proc/cpuinfo processor count=1.
linode_1_core.note: Deliberately the lightest/simplest apps per project convention (feedback_distributed_testing.md), so the shard leaves headroom for the server's live production request handling during the run.
